title: The Growing Danger of Lithium-Ion Battery Fires
subtitle: As rechargeable devices become part of daily life, fire services warn of a sharp rise in dangerous battery-related incidents.

summary.en: Lithium-ion batteries are now used in everything from mobile phones to electric bikes. However, new data shows that fires linked to these batteries are happening much more often in the UK. Experts warn that these fires are difficult to put out and can be very dangerous. They advise the public to use only certified products and follow safe charging habits. Better government rules are also needed to stop the sale of faulty or unsafe equipment.
summary.zh: 鋰離子電池目前被廣泛應用於從手機到電動自行車等各種設備中。然而，最新數據顯示，英國與這些電池相關的火災發生頻率大幅增加。專家警告，這類火災難以撲滅且極具危險性。他們建議大眾僅使用經過認證的產品，並養成安全的充電習慣。此外，政府也需要制定更完善的法規，以遏止劣質或不安全設備的銷售。

paragraphs.en: Lithium-ion batteries are everywhere in our modern world. They power the devices we use every day, including mobile phones, electric toothbrushes, toys, and vapes. They are also the power source for the growing number of electric bikes, e-scooters, and electric vehicles on our roads. While these batteries are useful, they have also created a new and serious safety risk. According to new data from the global business insurer QBE, fire brigades across the UK are now called to a lithium-ion battery fire roughly every five hours.
paragraphs.zh: 鋰離子電池在現代世界無處不在。它們為我們每天使用的設備提供動力，包括手機、電動牙刷、玩具和電子煙。它們也是道路上日益增加的電動自行車、電動滑板車和電動車的動力來源。雖然這些電池非常實用，但也帶來了全新的嚴重安全風險。根據全球商業保險公司昆士蘭保險集團（QBE）的最新數據，英國各地的消防隊平均每五小時就會接到一起與鋰離子電池相關的火災通報。

paragraphs.en: Research gathered by QBE shows that the problem is getting worse quickly. In 2025, fire services responded to 1,760 fires linked to these batteries. This is an increase of 147% over the last three years. The data, which includes information from 46 out of 52 fire brigades across the UK, highlights that these fires are not just a rare accident. They are becoming a common emergency that puts both people and property at risk.
paragraphs.zh: 昆士蘭保險集團收集的研究顯示，這個問題正迅速惡化。二〇二五年，消防單位處理了 1,760 起與這些電池有關的火災，這比過去三年增加了 147%。這份數據涵蓋了英國 52 個消防隊中的 46 個，凸顯出這些火災並非罕見的意外，而是正逐漸成為威脅民眾生命與財產安全的常見緊急事件。

paragraphs.en: One of the biggest concerns for fire experts is the rise of fires involving electric bikes, or ebikes. QBE researchers found that ebike fires made up nearly one-third of all lithium-ion battery fires across the country. The data shows that 520 such fires occurred in 2025, a large jump from 149 in 2022. Experts noted that ebikes that have been modified or converted by owners seem to be involved in more fires than certified, factory-made models. In London alone, the fire brigade dealt with 230 ebike fires last year, and there have been five deaths related to these incidents over the past three years.
paragraphs.zh: 消防專家最擔憂的問題之一是電動自行車火災的增加。昆士蘭保險集團的研究人員發現，電動自行車火災佔了全國所有鋰離子電池火災的近三分之一。數據顯示，二〇二五年發生了 520 起此類火災，較二〇二二年的 149 起大幅攀升。專家指出，由車主自行改裝的電動自行車，似乎比經過認證的原廠車型更容易引發火災。僅在倫敦，消防隊去年就處理了 230 起電動自行車火災，且過去三年內已有五人因這類事故喪生。

paragraphs.en: When these batteries are damaged or used incorrectly, they can cause a dangerous process called thermal runaway. This is a chain reaction where the temperature inside the battery rises without control. It creates a toxic gas that comes out at high pressure, which can lead to a fire that acts like a blowtorch and may even cause an explosion. Adrian Simmonds, a risk manager at QBE, explained that these fires are very difficult to fight. He noted that they burn differently than regular fires and can require up to ten times more water to put out safely.
paragraphs.zh: 當這些電池受損或使用不當時，可能會引發一種稱為「熱失控」的危險過程。這是一種連鎖反應，電池內部的溫度會失控上升。它會產生高壓噴出的有毒氣體，可能導致火勢像噴燈一樣燃燒，甚至引發爆炸。昆士蘭保險集團的風險經理亞德里安·西蒙茲（Adrian Simmonds）解釋說，這類火災非常難以撲滅。他指出，它們的燃燒方式與一般火災不同，可能需要多達十倍的水量才能安全撲滅。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the danger to homes—where nearly half of all these fires occur—there is also a major problem with how people throw these batteries away. Many people put them in normal trash bins, which is dangerous. When these batteries end up in bin lorries or recycling plants, they can start serious fires. These incidents cause injuries to workers and cost the economy more than £1 billion every year. Experts strongly advise that people should recycle their old batteries at special collection points, such as those found at many supermarkets, instead of throwing them in the trash.
paragraphs.zh: 除了對住家造成的危險（近半數此類火災發生於家中）之外，人們處理這些廢棄電池的方式也是一大問題。許多人將其丟入一般垃圾桶，這非常危險。當這些電池進入垃圾車或回收廠時，可能會引發嚴重的火災。這些事故不僅造成工作人員受傷，每年還對經濟造成超過十億英鎊的損失。專家強烈建議，民眾應將舊電池拿到超市等處設置的專用回收點進行回收，而不是直接丟進垃圾桶。

paragraphs.en: Fire officials are now calling for better government regulation to improve product safety. Spencer Sutcliff, a deputy commissioner for the London Fire Brigade, said that the brigade remains very concerned about the situation. He believes that new rules could help stop the sale of faulty or counterfeit products, such as cheap chargers or unsafe conversion kits, which are often sold on online marketplaces. A spokesperson for the National Fire Chiefs Council agreed, noting that the current findings match what they are seeing on the ground during their daily work.
paragraphs.zh: 消防官員目前正呼籲政府加強監管，以提升產品安全性。倫敦消防隊副局長史賓賽·薩特克利夫（Spencer Sutcliff）表示，消防隊對此情況仍深感憂慮。他認為，新法規有助於遏止劣質或仿冒產品的銷售，例如常在網路市集販售的廉價充電器或不安全的改裝套件。英國消防首長協會（National Fire Chiefs Council）的發言人對此表示認同，並指出目前的調查結果與他們在日常工作中觀察到的情況相符。

paragraphs.en: While waiting for new laws, experts say that public awareness is the best way to stay safe. Adrian Simmonds suggests that people should only buy certified ebikes and batteries from trusted sellers. He also warns that people should never charge their devices near escape routes, such as front doors or hallways, and should avoid charging them overnight while they are sleeping. By following these simple steps, individuals can help reduce the risk of a dangerous fire in their own homes. As the number of electric vehicles on the road continues to grow, understanding how to use and store these powerful batteries safely has never been more important.
paragraphs.zh: 在等待新法規出爐的同時，專家表示提升公眾意識是確保安全最好的方法。西蒙茲建議，民眾應只向值得信賴的賣家購買經過認證的電動自行車與電池。他也警告，民眾切勿在逃生路線（如前門或走廊）附近為設備充電，並應避免在睡覺時整夜充電。透過遵循這些簡單的步驟，個人可以協助降低家中發生危險火災的風險。隨著道路上的電動車數量持續增加，了解如何安全使用與存放這些強大的電池，變得前所未有的重要。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To form or constitute a part of a whole.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 組成、構成（某整體的一部分）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「編造（故事）」或「和好」，這裡指構成比例。文中：QBE researchers found that ebike fires made up nearly one-third of all lithium-ion battery fires across the country.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To take action to solve a problem or handle a situation.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「與某人打交道」，這裡指消防隊處理火災事故。文中：In London alone, the fire brigade dealt with 230 ebike fires last year, and there have been five deaths related to these incidents over the past three years.

keyTerms.definitionEn: In the actual place where an event is happening, rather than in an office or at a distance.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 在現場、在第一線（指實際運作的地方）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面上是「在地面上」，這裡指實際工作現場。文中：A spokesperson for the National Fire Chiefs Council agreed, noting that the current findings match what they are seeing on the ground during their daily work.

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: UK firefighters called to one lithium-ion battery fire every five hours

quiz.question: According to the research by QBE, what is a key factor that increases the risk of fires in electric bikes?

options.A: The bikes are being used for too many hours every day.
options.B: The bikes have been modified or converted by their owners.
options.C: The bikes are being charged in public spaces instead of homes.
options.D: The bikes are primarily manufactured by uncertified companies in London.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，專家指出由車主自行改裝（modified or converted）的電動自行車比原廠認證的車型更容易引發火災。選項 A 文章未提及使用時數；選項 C 文章提到火災多發生於家中而非公共場所；選項 D 的敘述過於籠統，文章強調的是改裝行為而非特定的製造商地點。

quiz.question: Based on the article, why is it particularly dangerous to dispose of lithium-ion batteries in standard household trash?

options.A: They are too heavy and can damage the mechanical arms of garbage trucks.
options.B: They contain toxic chemicals that leak into the soil at landfills.
options.C: They can be crushed or damaged during collection, triggering a chain reaction.
options.D: They are highly flammable when exposed to direct sunlight in trash bins.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第五段提到電池進入垃圾車或回收廠時，若受損可能引發嚴重火災。結合第四段對「熱失控」（thermal runaway）的描述，可知電池受損會導致連鎖反應，因此將其丟入一般垃圾中，在運輸或處理過程中極易因擠壓受損而引發火災。其他選項如重量問題、土壤污染或陽光直射均非文章提及的危險主因。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the phrase 'acts like a blowtorch' suggest about the nature of a lithium-ion battery fire?

options.A: It is a fire that is easy to extinguish with standard water spray.
options.B: It is a fire that produces a very intense, concentrated, and powerful flame.
options.C: It is a fire that slowly spreads across a wide surface area.
options.D: It is a fire that is caused by a lack of oxygen in the surrounding environment.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文中用 blowtorch（噴燈）來形容火災，意指其火焰極其強烈、集中且具有毀滅性。這與後文提到需要十倍的水量才能撲滅相呼應，顯示其燃燒強度極高。選項 A 與文章描述的困難撲滅矛盾；選項 C 描述的是緩慢燃燒，與噴燈的特性不符；選項 D 則是對物理現象的錯誤推論。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding lithium-ion batteries?

options.A: Lithium-ion batteries are becoming obsolete and should be replaced by safer energy sources.
options.B: The government has already successfully regulated the sale of all unsafe battery products.
options.C: While lithium-ion batteries are useful, they pose significant safety risks that require better regulation and public caution.
options.D: The increase in battery fires is primarily due to people failing to clean their devices regularly.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文旨在探討鋰離子電池在現代社會的普及與其帶來的嚴重安全隱憂。文章從數據分析、火災成因（熱失控）、錯誤處理方式到專家建議（政府監管與個人防範），全面性地闡述了這個議題，強調在享受便利的同時，必須透過法規與正確的使用習慣來降低風險。其他選項均與文章主旨不符或過度簡化。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
